customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, transactional posture. Reginald is motivated by bill pain ($1,100–$1,300/month with Dominion) and cooperative throughout. No scam skepticism, no hostility. Primary risk is calendar rigidity — he has a structured schedule and accepted the Monday 9:30 slot after checking his calendar explicitly.
unresolved_concerns: Wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment. She has not been introduced to the process and may have veto power at close.

Reginald accepted the Monday 9:30 slot after expressing a 30-minute time constraint on Friday. The closer should confirm at the top of the call that the appointment is a phone call and ask if the full hour works — Reginald may still be operating under a time constraint assumption.
Credit qualifier answered with 'Yes. Probably right' — soft hedge, not a firm confirmation. Closer should be prepared for a credit pull that may require a conversation.
sensitive_topics_raised: None. No vulnerability disclosures, no scam references, no financial hardship beyond the bill figure.
exact_promises_rep_made: Confirmation email with date and time of the meeting will be sent (tied to bill receipt — bill was received in-call at 09:37).
The specialist will review the design with Reginald on the call.
closer_should_know: The wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment — this is the primary structural risk. Confirm at the top of the call whether she is available to join. If she is not, the closer should decide whether to proceed or reschedule with both title-holders present. Secondary: Reginald's bill is $1,100–$1,300/month with Dominion — this is the emotional anchor the setter never flipped. The closer has a clean opening to lead with it.
